Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to produce a Python dictionary representing the temporal profile for the given supply chain process

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The scenario describes a process with activities SS OP RC QI CA PT PK WS DT AS

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The temporal profile considers all pairs that eventually follow each other in at least one process execution

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The process as described is sequential SS OP RC QI CA PT PK WS DT AS

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In a linear sequence the pairs are all combinations where the first appears before the second

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: The prompt says representative subset so we should include many pairs maybe all pairs but not necessarily all possible pairs

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We need to generate plausible numbers based on typical supply chain durations

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets think about typical durations for each activity in seconds

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Supplier Selection could take several days maybe average 5 days Order Placement maybe 1 day Receive Components maybe 2 weeks Quality Inspection maybe a day or two Component Assembly maybe 3 days Product Testing maybe 2 days Packaging maybe 1 day Warehouse Storage maybe 5 days Distribution maybe 2 days

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After Sales Support occurs after sale and the time between DT and AS could be months later so the interval could be large

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For non consecutive pairs the average time is the sum of average times between consecutive activities along the path

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We can define consecutive intervals such as SS to OP 6 hours OP to RC 14 days RC to QI 1 day QI to CA 1 day CA to PT 2 days PT to PK 1 day PK to WS 1 day WS to DT 5 days DT to AS 30 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If we assume the intervals are independent then the variance of a non consecutive pair is the sum of the variances and the standard deviation is the square root of that sum

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This would be consistent and realistic if we assume independence

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Given that the prompt asks for a representative subset we can choose all consecutive pairs and some longer range pairs from SS to later activities

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I will include all consecutive pairs and the non consecutive pairs from SS to RC SS to QI SS to CA SS to PT SS to PK SS to WS SS to DT SS to AS

Name: Validation - PE
Text: That set includes pairs separated by up to 9 steps so it is complex enough

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now we compute the averages and standard deviations for each selected pair using the additive model

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We should double check that the averages for non consecutive pairs are indeed sums of the consecutive ones

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The final dictionary is temporal profile with entries for consecutive pairs and the selected SS based non consecutive pairs
